The fix: add a step to ApprovePlan that parses plan.md for ## Bead N: ... sections, creates child beads under the implement molecule via bd create --parent, wires inter-bead dependencies from the Depends on lines, and writes generated bead IDs back into plan.md frontmatter.

Relevant code:
- internal/approve/plan.go (ApprovePlan)
- internal/validate/plan.go (already parses bead sections for validation)
- cmd/mindspec/approve.go (approve plan subcommand)
title: ApprovePlan should auto-create implementation beads from plan sections

description: When running 'mindspec complete' from a worktree, findRoot() resolves back to the main repo root via FindRoot() worktree resolution. This means complete.Run() cannot find the spec lifecycle.yaml because it only exists on the spec branch in the worktree, not in the main repo. Root cause: findRoot() in cmd/mindspec/root.go calls workspace.FindRoot() which resolves worktrees to the main repo. Same class of bug as the one fixed in f5576df for spec file resolution.

close_reason: Fixed: verifyImplContent now auto-merges unmerged bead branches into the spec branch during impl-approve instead of erroring
description:
During spec 053 impl-approve, the PR was created from spec/053-drop-state-json but implementation commits lived on bead/mindspec-mol-07lst (a child worktree branch). The PR only had 9 commits (spec+plan phase) instead of all 18 (including 6 bead implementation commits + fixes).

Root cause: approve/impl.go pushes the spec branch and creates the PR, but never merges the bead worktree branch(es) back into the spec branch first. The bead branches are children of the spec branch (branched from it), so their commits need to be merged up before the PR is created.

Fix: In ApproveImpl (or in the complete flow), before pushing spec branch and creating the PR, discover and merge all bead branches (bead/<beadID>) that descend from spec/<specID> into the spec branch. This should happen automatically — the user had to manually stash+merge+push to fix it.

Reproduction: any spec with bead worktrees (the standard flow since spec 048).
title: impl-approve fails to merge bead branches back to spec branch before PR

description:
MindSpec spec lifecycle: spec → approve → plan → approve → implement → review.

This formula tracks the full lifecycle of a single specification from drafting
through implementation and final review. Human gates ensure quality checkpoints
between phases.

## Usage

Create a molecule for a new spec:
```bash
bd mol wisp create spec-lifecycle --var spec_id=039-my-feature
```

Or for an existing spec that's already in progress:
```bash
bd mol wisp create spec-lifecycle --var spec_id=038-beads-native-multi-spec-state
```

## Artifacts Produced

Each phase creates or updates files under `.mindspec/docs/specs/047-clean-context-enforcement/`:
- `spec.md` — the specification (Phase 1)
- `context-pack.md` — generated context bundle (Phase 2, on approval)
- `plan.md` — the implementation plan (Phase 3)
- `proofs/` — optional proof outputs (Phase 5)
title: [SPEC 047-clean-context-enforcement] Clean Context Enforcement for Bead Starts

description: When an agent runs git worktree add from inside an existing worktree (e.g. .worktrees/worktree-spec-044/), the new worktree is created relative to that CWD rather than the project root. Guidance should warn agents to use absolute paths or run from the project root. Alternatively, mindspec next should always use absolute paths for worktree creation.
